faqs.q: Are Andrology and Urology services available across Yashoda Hospitals in Hyderabad?
faqs.a: Yashoda Hospitals in Hyderabad offer comprehensive Urology and Andrology services through dedicated Institutes of Urology and Andrology. Advanced technologies such as robotic-assisted and laparoscopic surgery are used to provide specialized care for male reproductive health, infertility, urinary tract disorders, and urological cancers, and expert multidisciplinary teams and modern infrastructure support. Andrology (male infertility, sexual health, erectile dysfunction, prostate disorders), urology (kidney stones, UTIs, female and reconstructive urology), uro-oncology (cancers of the kidney, bladder, prostate, and testicles), advanced minimally invasive endourology procedures (RIRS, HOLEP, PCNL, and TURP), pediatric urology, and renal transplantation are all included in the scope of services.

faqs.q: Who needs andrology treatment?
faqs.a: Andrology treatment is required for men with fertility issues such as difficulty conceiving after a year, low sperm count or quality, varicocele, sexual dysfunction, including erectile dysfunction, ejaculation disorders, low libido, penile abnormalities, hormonal issues such as low testosterone, prostate-related problems, or those seeking fertility preservation before cancer treatment or gender-affirming care. When conception does not occur within 12 months (or sooner if over 35), or when symptoms such as erectile dysfunction, testicular alterations, chronic exhaustion, hormonal symptoms, or urine changes appear, a medical checkup is indicated.

faqs.q: What are the common andrology issues?
faqs.a: Common andrology disorders include sexual dysfunction, such as erectile dysfunction and premature ejaculation, which can be caused by medical illnesses like diabetes or heart disease, as well as psychological causes like anxiety and stress. Male infertility and hormonal abnormalities are also common, such as poor sperm count or quality, low testosterone (hypogonadism), and varicocele, all of which can affect fertility, energy levels, and sexual health. Andrology treatment includes benign prostatic hyperplasia, prostatitis, prostate cancer, testicular diseases, urinary tract infections, and kidney stones.

faqs.q: What are the common Andrology services offered at Yashoda Hospitals, Hyderabad?
faqs.a: Yashoda Hospitals in Hyderabad evaluates and treats male infertility, erectile dysfunction, sexual health issues, prostate disorders, and uro-oncological conditions of the kidney, bladder, testis, and prostate. The department treats urinary stones, UTIs, and bladder problems with RIRS, PCNL, URSL, and laser-based treatments, including HoLEP. Andrology care is also supported by robotic surgery, laparoscopic, and endoscopic methods for prostatectomy and partial nephrectomy.

faqs.q: Is surgery necessary for all andrology conditions?
faqs.a: Surgery is optional for some andrology disorders since therapy is customized to the patient’s diagnosis, severity, underlying cause, and reproductive aspirations. Many diseases can be efficiently addressed non-invasively, including lifestyle changes, drugs, hormone treatment, antibiotics, and early medical care of erectile dysfunction and Peyronie’s disease. Assisted reproductive methods such as IUI, IVF, or ICSI may be used to treat male infertility without surgery. When conservative therapies fail or are insufficient, the surgical approach is reserved for structural or serious conditions such as varicocele, obstructive infertility, advanced erectile dysfunction, or emergencies such as testicular torsion.

faqs.q: How does Yashoda Hospital ensure patient comfort during andrology treatment?
faqs.a: Yashoda Hospitals prioritizes patient comfort during andrology treatment with a patient-centered approach that combines minimally invasive technology, customized care, and clear communication, reducing discomfort, anxiety, and recovery time. What are the common conditions treated by audiologists and speech therapists?

faqs.q: Why Choose Yashoda Hospitals for Audiology & Speech Therapy?
faqs.a: Best Hospital for Audiology & Speech Therapy Care: The audiologists and speech-language pathologists at the Centre for Ear, Nose, and Throat have excellent qualifications. This staff is among the best in the area, capable of handling emergencies, communication issues, and complex hearing impairments while providing both standard and advanced therapy regularly.

faqs.q: What is audiology, and how does it relate to speech therapy?
faqs.a: Audiology focuses on diagnosing and treating hearing, balance, and tinnitus issues for patients of all ages using tools like hearing aids and cochlear implants. Because clear hearing is vital for communication, audiologists frequently collaborate with speech-language pathologists who provide therapy for swallowing, language comprehension, and speech development.

faqs.q: What are the common conditions treated by audiologists and speech therapists?
faqs.a: While audiology addresses hearing loss, balance disorders, and ear-related conditions such as tinnitus or infections, speech-language pathology manages articulation, swallowing, and cognitive-communication issues, with both fields frequently intersecting to treat developmental delays and communication challenges related to hearing loss or autism.

faqs.q: What are the symptoms of hearing loss that I should be aware of?
faqs.a: Common warning signs of hearing loss include frequently asking others to repeat themselves, having difficulty with noisy environments, or requiring high television volumes, while specific red flags, such as “mumbling” perceptions in adults and delayed speech or lack of responsiveness in children, frequently lead to social withdrawal and developmental challenges.

faqs.q: How are hearing and speech disorders diagnosed, and what tests are used?
faqs.a: To diagnose hearing and balance problems, audiologists employ specialized instruments, such as pure-tone audiometry, tympanometry, and auditory brainstem responses, to assess everything from sound sensitivity to middle-ear and cochlear function, particularly in babies. This precise hearing profile then enables speech-language pathologists to correctly assess communication and swallowing abilities, resulting in a highly individualized therapy plan.

faqs.q: What treatments are available for hearing loss in adults and children?
faqs.a: Hearing loss is treated using custom-programmed hearing aids, cochlear implants, or bone-anchored devices, depending on the severity and kind of impairment. In circumstances when persistent infections or structural difficulties exist, ENT surgeons may need to perform medicinal or surgical procedures in addition to assistive equipment such as FM systems for classrooms. To maximize the usefulness of these devices, speech-language pathologists give critical therapy focusing on speech-sound development for children and communication coping skills for adults.

faqs.q: What therapies are effective for speech delays in children?
faqs.a: Effective speech therapies are tailored to a child’s specific needs using articulation and oral-motor exercises to strengthen facial muscles and improve sound production through repetitive play. Language and play-based interventions utilize storytelling and toys to encourage vocabulary building, sentence structure, and active communication choices in a natural setting. For non-verbal children, specialists may introduce augmentative communication tools while simultaneously training parents in techniques to narrate daily activities and reinforce learning at home.

faqs.q: When should I see a specialist for my child’s speech development?
faqs.a: Because a child’s brain is most malleable to language before the age of five, parents should seek help if their 12-month-old isn’t employing gestures or responding to their name. Other warning signs include an 18-month-old who avoids vocalizing, a 2-year-old with a limited vocabulary, or any youngster who abruptly loses previously learned speech abilities.

faqs.a: Specialized Geriatric Care & Expertise: We utilize non-pharmacological treatments, sophisticated diagnostics such as DEXA scans, and thorough geriatric evaluations to achieve better outcomes in restoring independence and reversing frailty.

faqs.q: What is geriatric care, and how does it differ from general healthcare?
faqs.a: Geriatric care is a crucial subspecialty of medicine that focuses on the specific health requirements of older persons, generally those aged 65 and over. In general healthcare, diseases are usually treated separately, whereas senior care looks at the whole person and how different medicines and chronic conditions affect each other. Rather than simply focusing on clinical indicators such as blood pressure or laboratory results, geriatricians prioritize “functional health,” the capacity to complete daily tasks and maintain independence.

faqs.q: What are the common conditions treated by geriatric specialists?
faqs.a: Geriatric specialists typically treat “geriatric syndromes,” which are complicated illnesses that may not necessarily have a clear explanation. These include frailty, frequent falls, urine incontinence, and sleep difficulties, as well as chronic illnesses such as cardiovascular disease and type 2 diabetes. They also focus on cognitive and bone health, addressing illnesses including dementia, Alzheimer’s, and osteoporosis.

faqs.q: What are the symptoms of dementia, and how can they be managed?
faqs.a: Progressive memory loss, confusion about time and place, and difficulty forming words are early signs of dementia, which is characterized by a decline in cognitive function that affects daily life. As the condition advances, individuals may experience personality changes, mood swings, and a lack of motor coordination. Management involves maintaining a stable environment, using memory aids, and occasionally giving medication like cholinesterase inhibitors to temporarily slow the progression of symptoms.

faqs.q: What treatments are available for arthritis in the elderly?
faqs.a: The goal of arthritis care for older persons is to reduce pain, preserve joint function, and retain independence using a multimodal approach. This technique combines lifestyle changes, including low-impact exercise and weight control, with targeted medicinal treatments ranging from topical analgesics to sophisticated disease-modifying drugs. Low-impact workouts (such as swimming) and weight control are essential for decreasing joint stress, while physical therapy aids in maintaining the range of motion. Topical NSAID gels (which have less systemic adverse effects than tablets), acetaminophen for pain, and biologics or DMARDs for inflammatory conditions such as rheumatoid arthritis are among the medical choices.

faqs.q: How is depression in older adults treated?
faqs.a: Depression in seniors is often treated with a mix of psychotherapy, such as Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT), and medications. Because older persons may be more vulnerable to side effects and potential medication interactions, doctors frequently prescribe lower dosages of antidepressants, such as SSRIs. Addressing social isolation using community participation and regular physical exercise is also an important component of a holistic treatment plan.

faqs.q: What surgical procedures might be recommended for elderly patients?
faqs.a: Elderly adults routinely have elective treatments to improve mobility and quality of life, with hip and knee arthroplasty (replacements) being among the most popular. Other popular surgeries include cataract surgery to restore eyesight, hernia repairs, and heart procedures such as pacemaker insertion or valve replacement. Surgeons use minimally invasive procedures wherever possible to decrease tissue stress and promote speedier healing.

faqs.q: What is the recovery process like after surgery for older adults?
faqs.a: Recovery for older persons necessitates a systematic, multidisciplinary approach, which frequently begins with early movement to prevent muscular atrophy and blood clots. Post-operative treatment often involves extensive physical and occupational therapy tailored to the patient’s basic physical health to restore functional independence. Because senior patients are more likely to develop problems such as delirium or infections, continuous monitoring in the hospital and a carefully organized transfer to home or rehabilitation facilities are required.

Interventional cardiology treats important illnesses involving coronary arteries, heart valves, and vasculature, including ACS, stable angina, aortic stenosis, mitral regurgitation, and PAD. faqs.q: What is the difference between an interventional cardiologist and a cardiothoracic surgeon?
faqs.a: Interventional cardiologists are experts at applying minimally invasive procedures through the wrist or groin to treat coronary blockages and valve problems without opening the chest. Their specialized training lets them carry out high-precision operations such as angioplasty, stenting, and structural repairs like TAVR. Cardiothoracic surgeons specialize in open-heart surgery, which may include the use of heart-lung equipment to complete complex surgeries such as CABG, heart transplants, and structural valve replacements. Their deep expertise in general and cardiothoracic surgery enables them to treat complicated mechanical heart diseases requiring direct access to the chest cavity.

faqs.q: What should I expect during an interventional cardiology consultation?
faqs.a: During a consultation, your cardiologist will do a thorough physical exam and medical history review to detect symptoms and risk factors, frequently supported by diagnostic tools like an EKG or blood test. Following the examination, the doctor will explain potential treatment paths, including minimally invasive catheter-based treatments, and build a clear plan for your future steps and specialized follow-up care.

faqs.q: What tests will the interventional cardiologists at Yashoda Hospital use before treatment?
faqs.a: Yashoda Hospitals uses blood testing, EKGs, cardiac MRI, and high-resolution coronary CT angiography. During cardiac catheterization, physicians use FFR, IVUS, and OCT to assess plaque and optimize stent placement. The department also offers electrophysiology examinations to map the heart’s electrical circuitry and stress testing to assess performance during exercise. These diverse skills, backed by cutting-edge Cath labs, guarantee that every patient receives a highly precise and personalized treatment plan for their unique cardiac condition.

faqs.q: Is angioplasty the same as heart surgery?
faqs.a: Angioplasty is a minimally invasive procedure (catheter-based), but CABG is a major open-heart surgery requiring a chest incision. Angioplasty patients often go home in 1-2 days and return to work in a week, whereas CABG needs a while in the hospital and weeks for healing. Angioplasty forces plaque against the arterial wall, while CABG completely bypasses obstructions. Local anesthetic is commonly used during angioplasty, and general anesthesia is required for bypass surgery.

faqs.q: What technologies are used in interventional cardiology?
faqs.a: Core technological advancements used in interventional cardiology include robotic-assisted systems, artificial intelligence, machine learning, optical coherence technology, intravascular ultrasound, drug-eluting stents, transcatheter mitral valve repair, TAVR, 3D imaging, rotational angiography, and nanotechnology drug delivery systems.

faqs.q: What is neonatology, and what conditions do neonatologists treat?
faqs.a: Neonatology is the medical treatment of babies, particularly those who are preterm or unwell in their first 28 days. Neonatologists diagnose and treat illnesses such as preterm birth, respiratory distress syndrome (RDS), newborn jaundice, infections, congenital heart abnormalities, birth asphyxia, and metabolic problems.

faqs.q: What are the common symptoms of neonatal conditions that require medical attention?
faqs.a: Poor eating, lethargy, quick or difficult breathing, jaundice (yellow skin), fever or hypothermia, convulsions, vomiting, diarrhea, or unusual cries are common characteristics of neonatal disorders. These need to be addressed immediately to avoid consequences such as organ failure.

faqs.q: How is a neonatal condition diagnosed in infants?
faqs.a: A comprehensive history, physical exam, monitoring of vital signs, and observation in the NICU are the first steps in determining the diagnosis. To ensure correct identification, it combines clinical assessment with blood testing, imaging, and specialist consultations.

faqs.q: What diagnostic tests are commonly used in neonatology?
faqs.a: Common diagnostics include blood testing (CBC, CRP, cultures), heel-prick screening, X-rays, ultrasounds (head, heart, belly), echocardiograms, EEG for seizures, and molecular PCR for infections. Metabolic screening for newborns detects genetic abnormalities early in life.

faqs.q: What treatments are available for premature infants?
faqs.a: Premature newborns receive NICU treatments that include breathing assistance (CPAP, ventilators), temperature management (incubators), IV feeding, antibiotics for infections, and pulmonary medicines, such as surfactant. Developmental therapy and monitoring promote long-term development.

faqs.q: What are the treatment options for neonatal jaundice?
faqs.a: Mild jaundice goes away on its own; phototherapy (blue light) is commonly used to break down bilirubin. Exchange transfusions or intravenous immunoglobulin therapy may be required in difficult situations.

faqs.q: How is respiratory distress syndrome treated in newborns?
faqs.a: RDS is treated with exogenous surfactant via endotracheal tube, CPAP or mechanical breathing, oxygen treatment, and prenatal steroids for high-risk mothers. Antibiotics and nutritional supplementation are examples of supportive treatment.

faqs.q: Can a vitamin deficiency cause foot pain?
faqs.a: Foot pain is frequently caused by a lack of vitamin D, which produces agonizing bone pain, or a deficiency in B-complex vitamins (B1, B6, and B12), which causes nerve burning and tingling. Additionally, a shortage of magnesium and calcium can lead to muscular weakness and illnesses such as plantar fasciitis. To address these symptoms, see a doctor for blood tests before beginning any dietary modifications or supplements, such as high-dose B12 or multivitamins.

faqs.q: Can your feet give an early warning about serious health problems?
faqs.a: Because your feet are the furthest from your heart, they frequently show the earliest indications of systemic disorders, including diabetes, heart disease, and peripheral artery disease, such as numbness, coldness, or slow-healing sores. Certain bodily changes, such as unexplained swelling, hair loss on the toes, or skin discoloration, may serve as early warning signs of severe problems, including renal failure, poor circulation, or thyroid abnormalities. Persistent foot soreness should not be overlooked, as a professional review of these “early warning signs” can lead to a quick diagnosis and avert serious long-term problems.

faqs.q: Is podiatry covered by health insurance?
faqs.a: At Yashoda Hospitals, our expert physiotherapists, orthopedic surgeons, and reconstructive microsurgeons look forward to discussing your options and helping you make informed decisions. Additionally, healthcare insurance may partially cover the cost of artificial disc replacement surgery, so it’s essential to check with your insurance provider. You can also explore government subsidies or policies that may help you manage various ADR surgery costs at Yashoda Hospitals in India.

faqs.q: What foot problems do children need podiatry for?
faqs.a: Pediatric podiatry examines developmental foot disorders such as flat feet, gait deviations such as toe walking, and congenital deformities to enable correct biomechanical growth. Custom orthotics, exercises, and footwear advice are used by specialists to treat common juvenile disorders such as painful ingrown toenails, plantar warts, and sports-related injuries such as Sever’s disease. To avoid long-term mobility difficulties, parents should seek professional assistance if their child is experiencing persistent discomfort, frequent stumbling, or a noticeable change in their walking pattern.

faqs.q: How long does it take to recover from common foot treatments?
faqs.a: For mild sprains, it can take two to four weeks for the foot to recover. For significant fractures or reconstructive surgery, it can take several months. Sometimes, the swelling can linger up to four months. Rest, elevation, and the use of prescription orthopedic boots or physical therapy are all critical components of successful healing. Early professional intervention and advanced surgical procedures may significantly accelerate this process, allowing some patients to resume carrying weights much quicker than old approaches permitted.

faqs.q: How can I prevent recurring foot problems?
faqs.a: To keep your feet healthy, wear supportive, well-fitting footwear and practice everyday cleanliness, such as cleaning and drying thoroughly between your toes to prevent infection. To avoid ingrown toenails, cut them straight across and engage in low-impact activities or stretching to strengthen your feet while maintaining your body weight. It is critical to avoid going barefoot and to examine your feet regularly for any wounds, redness, or non-healing sores that may need expert treatment. Consulting a podiatrist is vital for safe and efficient treatment of recurrent concerns such as corns, calluses, or trouble with nail care, particularly for individuals with diabetes.

faqs.q: What is proctology, and what conditions do proctologists treat?
faqs.a: Proctology is concerned with identifying and treating problems of the anus, rectum, colon, and sigmoid. Proctologists, often known as colorectal surgeons, treat disorders such as hemorrhoids , anal fissures , fistulas , abscesses, diverticulitis, polyps, colorectal cancer, and inflammatory bowel disease .

faqs.q: What are the common symptoms of hemorrhoids?
faqs.a: Common symptoms include bright red blood after bowel movements, itching or irritation around the anus, and pain or discomfort, particularly in cases of external or thrombosed hemorrhoids. If the anus prolapses, a sensitive lump may form near it.

faqs.q: What are the symptoms of diverticulitis?
faqs.a: Sudden intense stomach discomfort, usually on the lower left side, is accompanied by fever , chills , nausea, vomiting , diarrhea , or constipation . Pain might develop over time and fluctuate in severity.

faqs.q: What are the symptoms of colorectal polyps?
faqs.a: Polyps seldom produce symptoms; however, they might cause rectal bleeding, changes in bowel habits such as diarrhea or constipation, stomach pains, bloating , or unexplained lethargy due to anemia.

faqs.q: What diagnostic tests are used to evaluate rectal bleeding?
faqs.a: A physical exam, digital rectal exam, and anoscopy are the first steps in the evaluation process; other tests may include a colonoscopy, sigmoidoscopy, fecal occult blood test, or imaging such as a CT scan , if necessary.

faqs.q: How is colorectal cancer diagnosed?
faqs.a: Colonoscopy is used for visualization/ biopsy , stool testing for blood/DNA, CT / MRI scans , blood tests such as CEA , and in certain cases, PET scans or endoscopy.

faqs.q: How is a colonoscopy performed, and what should I expect?
faqs.a: Colonoscopy is putting a flexible tube with a camera into the anus to view the colon following bowel preparation with laxatives and a clear liquid diet. Sedation and minor cramps are to be expected throughout the 20-60 minute surgery, as well as air inflation for a better view and biopsy/polyp removal if necessary; recovery takes 30-60 minutes with light activity that day.

Arthroscopy is a minimally invasive surgical procedure used to diagnose and treat various joint conditions. It is particularly effective for knee issues, such as torn ligaments, cartilage, or meniscus, and swollen joint linings, as well as shoulder problems, including rotator cuff tears and recurrent dislocations. Additionally, arthroscopy can address other conditions like arthritis, Baker’s cyst, carpal tunnel syndrome, frozen shoulder, bone spurs, synovitis, and temporomandibular disorder (TMD).

Arthroscopy is used to treat various sports injuries, including knee issues like ACL tears, meniscal tears, and instability, as well as shoulder problems such as rotator cuff tears and dislocations. It also addresses conditions like tennis elbow, ankle sprains, and fractures.

Our weight loss programs are a mix of most advanced knowledge and technology, extensive experience, and highest level of compassion.

At Yashoda Hospitals, our multi-disciplinary bariatric surgery team works closely with endocrinologists , nutiritonists, lifestyle counselors, dieticians and exercise specialists to offer a presonalized weight loss program. The weight loss program is carefully designed to assess the individual’s condition and customize the treatment plan and surgical procedure for successful outcomes. The holistic approach includes nutritional management, counseling, wellness program and surgical management to combat any type of weight & its related syndromes.

Cardiologists , pulmonologists , anesthetists work closely with the bariatric surgeons to understand the person’s health status and coexisting diseases. Together they offer personalized care to our patients throughout the journey – assessment, planning, preparation before surgery, during surgery and recovery after surgery for positive and long lasting outcomes.

At Yashoda Hospitals, we specialize in advanced bariatric surgeries – adjustable gastric banding , sleeve gastrectomy , roux-en-y gastric bypass, biliopancreatic diversion with duodenal switch and revisional bariatric procedures using latest technologies:

Robotic surgery : Using robotics for bariatric surgery offers undeniable benefits of small surgical scars, reduced post-operative pain and faster recovery. Our doctors perform robotic surgeries in patients who are more than 250 kgs and are not eligible for other techniques such as SILS, and laparoscopy.

Single incision laparoscopic surgery (SILS): SILS is the next generation laparoscopy wherein a single incision as small as 5 to 12 mm is made just at or above the belly-button. SILS gastric banding offers benefits over laparoscopy- lesser pain and no visible scar.

Bariatric surgery—the life-altering procedure—has gained paramount importance in recent times and has emerged as an important tool for managing severe obesity and conditions associated with it.
Robotic surgery has grown in popularity, with modern technology and robotic equipment assisting surgeons in a variety of treatments

Sleeve Gastrectomy and Gastric Bypass are two commonly performed bariatric surgeries. Higher BMI can lead to serious weight-related health problems, such as type 2 diabetes, high blood pressure or severe sleep apnea.

Health Talk

Bariatric surgery offers numerous benefits, including significant weight loss, improved overall health by resolving obesity-related conditions like heart disease and type 2 diabetes, and enhanced quality of life through better mobility and breathing. It also reduces the risk of death by over 40% and improves emotional well-being, fertility, sleep apnea, osteoarthritis, and incontinence outcomes, making it a comprehensive treatment for individuals with obesity.
Diseases & Treatments

faqs.q: What is the bariatric surgery?
faqs.a: Bariatric surgery, or weight loss surgery, helps people with obesity by altering the digestive system to limit calorie intake and absorption, promoting weight loss and improved health.

faqs.q: Is bariatric surgery safe?
faqs.a: Bariatric surgery is a safe and effective option for long-term weight loss, especially for those with obesity. While it doesn’t replace a healthy diet and exercise, it serves as a valuable tool in managing weight.

faqs.q: Am I eligible for bariatric surgery?
faqs.a: You may be eligible for bariatric surgery if you have a BMI of 40 or higher, or 35-39.9 with a serious weight-related condition. Eligibility also depends on having no other underlying causes for obesity, no severe heart disease or blood-clotting disorders, and a commitment to adopting healthier eating habits and lifestyle changes, including following a preoperative program and avoiding excessive alcohol.

faqs.q: How long will bariatric surgery take?
faqs.a: Bariatric surgery typically lasts between one and three hours, depending on the procedure. While the surgery is relatively quick, the overall process includes preparation, the operation itself, and recovery.

faqs.q: What are the benefits of bariatric surgery?
faqs.a: Bariatric surgery offers numerous benefits, including significant weight loss, improved overall health by resolving obesity-related conditions like heart disease and type 2 diabetes, and enhanced quality of life through better mobility and breathing. It also reduces the risk of death by over 40% and improves emotional well-being, fertility, sleep apnea, osteoarthritis, and incontinence outcomes, making it a comprehensive treatment for individuals with obesity.

pages.title: Best Cardiology Hospital in Hyderabad
overview: At Yashoda Heart Institute, we are committed to providing unparalleled cardiac care through a comprehensive multidisciplinary approach. Known as one of the best Cardiology Hospitals in Hyderabad and among the top Heart hospitals in India, we offer extensive diagnostic, medical, and surgical cardiology services. Our state-of-the-art heart clinics and highly skilled team of cardiologists, surgeons, and healthcare professionals put us at the forefront of cardiac innovation. We provide advanced treatment and quality care, ensuring that each patient receives the best outcomes and is on the path to a healthy heart.
Successfully performed the first inter-state Heart Transplantation in Telangana and AP.
Performed the first-ever combined heart-lung transplantation successfully in Telangana and AP.
Recognized as one of the world’s leading Comprehensive Heart & Lung Transplantation Centers , achieving exceptional outcomes in complex procedures.
The first Dual Source CT with Heart PBV was introduced in India, enhancing diagnostic accuracy and patient care standards.
Collaborated with surgeons from Duke University, USA, to perform PTE procedures in Pulmonary Embolism, ensuring world-class treatment standards.
sectionHighlights: World-Class Facilities for Advanced Cardiac Care
Leader in Minimally Invasive Cardiac Surgeries
Offers state-of-the-art facilities for Ventricular Assist Devices (VADs), Pacemakers , and ICD Implantations, ensuring comprehensive cardiac care.
Pioneers in Awake ECMO procedures and Air Ambulance Transport Facilities , ensuring swift and effective critical care response.
Recognized for excellence in Complex Cardiothoracic and Minimally Invasive Cardiac Surgeries , offering advanced treatment for various heart and lung conditions.

Advanced Diagnostic Tools: We offer cutting-edge imaging technologies such as CT Coronary Angiogram , Cardiac MRI, and Radionuclide Studies. Our facilities also include the latest 2D/3D ECHO , TEE, DSE, Head-Up Tilt Test, HOLTER, and ABPM for comprehensive cardiac evaluation.

Specialized Cath Labs: Our Hybrid Cath Lab and dedicated Neuro Intervention Cath Lab are equipped with the most advanced tools, including flat-panel imaging technology, IVUS , OCT , NIRS IVUS, and 3D Mapping for precise diagnosis and treatment.

Innovative Treatment Options: Our department specializes in coronary, structural, and peripheral interventions, as well as electrophysiology diagnosis and intervention. We provide image-guided complex coronary interventions, including OCT /IVUS-guided procedures, Left Main and Bifurcation CTO treatments, Post-bypass Interventional Complex Angioplasty, and support with devices like Impella and PulseCath-assisted ECMO.

Cutting-Edge Procedures: We offer advanced treatments such as TAVR , TMVR, MITRA CLIP , LAA Appendage Closure, and the MICRA leadless pacemaker. Our electrophysiology team performs advanced procedures using 3D mapping for VT and atrial fibrillation, including renal denervation and advanced ETS & RFA .

Our doctors at Yashoda Heart Institute can diagnose and treat heart and vascular problems using the latest technology and skills that are unmatched skills. Our team of interventional cardiologists specialises in treating and preventing heart disease for any cardiovascular disease suspect seeking medical attention.

Yashoda Hospitals offers regular screening programs and heart health check-ups to ensure early detection and prevention of cardiac conditions. Our preventive and curative cardiology services are designed to maintain and improve heart health for all patients.
By combining advanced technology, specialized expertise, and a commitment to patient-centered care, Yashoda Hospitals stands out as a leader in the field of cardiology.

faqs.q: Why Choose Yashoda Hospitals?
faqs.a: The Cardiology Department at Yashoda Hospitals is equipped with state-of-the-art technologies and equipment, ensuring the highest standard of cardiac care.

faqs.q: Why is it performed?
faqs.a: The new 3.0 Tesla MRI, featuring biomatrix technology, delivers clear, high-resolution neuroimaging and oncology scans. People prefer 3.0 Tesla MRI because it enables the detection of smaller anatomical abnormalities that are otherwise difficult to find; is useful for precise cardiac function assessment and coronary and perfusion imaging for diagnosing and managing blocked arteries, ischaemic heart disease, heart muscle movements, and tumours.

faqs.q: Why is it performed?
faqs.a: Dual-source CT scans encompass advanced coronary angiography, superior temporal resolution, easily analyze plaque’s characterization, the elimination of the use of beta-blocker medicines, and dual-energy capabilities, with varying generations offering diverse features for numerous medical conditions. They are for people with rapid heart rates, excessive plaque accumulation, sensitivity toward radiation exposure, and countless other individuals in need.

faqs.q: Why is it performed?
faqs.a: Our skilled cardiologists, radiologists, and other medical specialists prefer diagnostic fluoroscopy because it offers real-time moving imaging for interventional cardiological treatments such as angioplasty, stent placement, heart blockages, valvular heart disease, and catheterization. It also allows visualizing blood flow throughout the heart and arteries and aiding in diagnostics and minimally invasive treatments.

faqs.q: Why is it performed?
faqs.a: Ultra-low angiography is considered for patients with pre-existing kidney diseases to minimize the risk of contrast-induced nephropathy by reducing the need for contrast usage. Consequently, ultra-low angiography is time efficient and allows thorough interventions such as intra-coronary imaging physiology for procedure guidance, thus enhancing patient safety.

faqs.q: Why is it performed?
faqs.a: Intravascular Ultrasound (IVUS) helps doctors treat people who are at risk of coronary artery, carotid artery, and peripheral artery diseases, heart attacks, or strokes. It uses sound waves to visualize the inside of the heart to provide a detailed assessment of the artery, provides guidance during interventional surgeries, and helps evaluate stent failure and examine plaque characterization.

faqs.q: Why is it performed?
faqs.a: Biplane cath labs can generate high-resolution 3D images, minimize radiation exposure and contrast dye usage, and facilitate faster, more efficient, minimally invasive cardiac procedures in a single session. Biplane cath labs are applicable for patients with neurovascular or cardiac disorders, such as arteriovenous malformation. Their unique capabilities make them particularly well-suited for diverse neurological, vascular, cardiac, and electrophysiological studies.

faqs.q: Why is it performed?
faqs.a: The DaVinci XI robotic system is the top choice for cardiologists in minimally invasive surgeries like coronary artery bypass grafting (CABG) and valve repair due to its enhanced 3D HD imaging for precision. DaVinci XI robotic technologies facilitate faster recovery, less pain, fewer complications, and better reconstructive results.

pages.title: Best Child Development Center in Hyderabad, India
overview: Every child grows at their own pace, reaching important milestones like sitting, walking, speaking, and interacting with the world around them. While many children progress smoothly, some may show delays or differences in development that parents begin to notice in everyday life such as, difficulty with speech, learning, walking, paying attention, or social interaction.

In today’s generation, conditions like Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) and Attention-Deficit/Hyperactivity Disorder (ADHD) are being recognized more often. Early awareness and timely support can make a world of difference in helping children achieve their fullest potential. That’s why it is important not only to track growth in terms of height and weight, but also to closely observe cognitive, emotional, and behavioral milestones. Our center for child development is designed to partner with parents at every step, offering not just medical expertise but also reassurance, resources, and hope for your child’s brighter future.
Specialised outpatient program designed to support children suffering from neurodevelopmental disorders:

At Yashoda Hospital’s Child Development Center, we provide specialized care dedicated to nurturing every aspect of your child’s growth. Under the guidance of expert Pediatric Neurologists , our centre brings together a multidisciplinary team including speech therapists, occupational therapists, physiotherapists, behavioral specialists, and special educators. This collaborative approach ensures that each child receives a personalized plan for their unique needs, whether it’s enhancing communication, improving motor skills, or supporting learning and behavior.

We understand that every child’s journey is unique, and some may need extra care and guidance to reach their full potential. Addressing this need, our Child Development Center, has carefully curated services like pediatric physiotherapy for gait and balance training, dedicated programs for developmental delay management and cerebral palsy rehabilitation through sensory integration therapy and a structured early intervention program, to help children build essential motor, sensory, and social skills at the right age.

For children with conditions like Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD), ADHD, and learning disabilities, various support programs that focus on communication, behavior, and academic readiness are available. We truly believe that parents play a central role in their child’s progress, which is why we provide ongoing parent counseling and guidance helping families understand, cope, and actively participate in their child’s development making us one of the best child development centres in Hyderabad.

Pediatric neurologists treat infants, children, and adolescents, while adult neurologists focus on adults. Pediatric neurologists account for brain development, as it affects how disorders present. Causes and treatments for neurological issues often differ between children and adults. Pediatric neurologists undergo specialized training to manage conditions like cerebral palsy, brain injuries, and developmental delays, while both pediatric and adult neurologists treat headaches, seizures, and epilepsy.

Common neurological disorders in children include ADHD, autism spectrum disorder (ASD), cerebral palsy, epilepsy, migraines, and developmental delays. Other conditions like brain injuries, concussions, encephalitis, learning disorders, metabolic disorders, and multiple sclerosis (MS) also affect children. Each disorder has unique symptoms and treatment approaches, making professional evaluation crucial if neurological symptoms are suspected.

Pediatric neurologists specialize in diagnosing and treating neurological, behavioral, and learning disorders in children, including ADHD, learning disabilities, seizures, epilepsy, muscle issues, headaches, autism, and developmental disorders. They are trained to manage conditions of the central and peripheral nervous system in children under 18 and help develop comprehensive treatment plans for conditions like ADHD and autism.

faqs.a: Common neurological disorders in children include ADHD, autism spectrum disorder (ASD), cerebral palsy, epilepsy, migraines, and developmental delays. Each disorder has unique symptoms and treatment approaches, making professional evaluation crucial if neurological symptoms are suspected.

faqs.q: What are the early signs of neurological disorders in infants and young children?
faqs.a: Early signs of neurological disorders in infants and young children include abnormal muscle tone, delayed developmental milestones, unusual eye movements, abnormal heart rate or breathing, feeding difficulties, seizures, behavior changes, and loss of previously mastered skills like walking or talking. Symptoms vary based on the affected area of the brain, spine, or nerves, making early diagnosis and intervention crucial.

In India, 34% of people with sepsis die in the intensive care unit. Globally, sepsis is attributed to causing deaths greater than those caused by heart diseases and stroke combined. Despite these grave consequences, fewer people know about sepsis. Early detection of infection and timely treatment can save lives.

Critical care, or intensive care, is specialized medical care for patients with life-threatening conditions, typically provided in a hospital’s ICU. It involves round-the-clock monitoring and treatment by a team of trained healthcare providers, including doctors, nurses, and respiratory therapists, using advanced machines to monitor vital signs.
Patients with heart conditions are admitted to the Coronary Care Unit (CCU) or Cardiac Intensive Care Unit (CICU) for round-the-clock monitoring and specialized treatment.
Diseases & Treatments

faqs.q: What is meant by critical care?
faqs.a: Critical care, or intensive care, is specialized medical care for patients with life-threatening conditions, typically provided in a hospital’s ICU. It involves round-the-clock monitoring and treatment by a team of trained healthcare providers, including doctors, nurses, and respiratory therapists, using advanced machines to monitor vital signs.

faqs.q: What is critical care medicine?
faqs.a: Critical care medicine specializes in diagnosing and treating patients with life-threatening illnesses or injuries.

faqs.q: What is the difference between ICU and critical care?
faqs.a: Critical care and the Intensive Care Unit (ICU) both refer to medical care provided to patients with life-threatening injuries or illnesses.

faqs.q: What kind of specialists work in critical care units?
faqs.a: ICU doctors, also known as intensivists, are specialists who have undergone advanced training in intensive care medicine or related fields like anesthetics, cardiology, or emergency medicine.

faqs.q: What kind of patients are taken to CCU?
faqs.a: Patients with heart conditions are admitted to the Coronary Care Unit (CCU) or Cardiac Intensive Care Unit (CICU) for round-the-clock monitoring and specialized treatment.

pages.title: Best Dental Hospital in Hyderabad
overview: At Yashoda Hospitals, we recognise that dental wellness is not just about a beautiful smile—it’s an integral part of your overall health and well-being. The mouth serves as the gateway to the body, and the state of your teeth and gums can profoundly influence various aspects of your health. Emerging research has consistently highlighted the intricate link between oral health and systemic health, revealing that poor oral hygiene can lead to serious health complications, such as cardiovascular diseases, diabetes , respiratory infections , and even complications during pregnancy.

Our Department of Dental Surgery is dedicated to placing oral health at the forefront of comprehensive care. We understand that achieving and maintaining optimal oral health requires a blend of expertise, cutting-edge technology, and a patient-centered approach. That’s why our team of highly experienced and skilled dental surgeons is committed to providing top-tier dental services, ensuring every patient receives the highest standard of care tailored to their unique needs.

In addition to routine dental care, our department excels in advanced smile correction techniques designed to enhance both the function and aesthetics of your smile. Whether it’s through the use of invisible braces or other state-of-the-art orthodontic solutions, we strive to align teeth to their most cosmetically pleasing position, helping our patients achieve a smile they can be proud of.

Our commitment to excellence in dental care ensures that every patient leaves with not just a healthy smile but also a renewed confidence in their overall well-being. At Yashoda Hospitals, your smile is our priority, and your health is our mission.

Skin diseases encompass a wide range of conditions affecting the skin, hair, and nails and can impact individuals of all ages. Diseases & Treatments

faqs.q: What is the meaning of endocrinology?
faqs.a: Endocrinology is the study of hormones and the glands and organs that secrete them within the endocrine system.

faqs.q: How does the endocrine system affect diabetes?
faqs.a: Diabetes is an endocrine disorder affecting the pancreas, which produces insulin. When the pancreas makes insufficient insulin or the body doesn’t use it properly, blood sugar levels rise, as insulin helps move sugar into cells for energy.

faqs.q: What is an endocrinology test for?
faqs.a: Endocrine tests are used to diagnose hormonal disorders and determine the best treatment plan. Physicians use various tests to measure hormone levels and identify endocrine issues.

faqs.q: How do you treat diabetes?
faqs.a: Managing diabetes involves several key strategies, as it affects everyone differently. The main aspects include monitoring blood sugar levels with a glucose meter or continuous glucose monitor (CGM), taking oral diabetes medications like metformin (mainly for Type 2 diabetes), and using insulin for those with Type 1 diabetes or advanced Type 2 diabetes. Diet plays a crucial role, with meal planning and carb counting for those on insulin, while regular exercise helps improve insulin sensitivity. Maintaining a healthy weight, blood pressure, and cholesterol is also essential to reducing the risk of heart disease.

faqs.q: What is the endocrine treatment for thyroid problems?
faqs.a: Treatment for thyroid disease depends on the condition and its cause. For hyperthyroidism, options include antithyroid drugs to stop hormone production, radioiodine therapy to damage thyroid cells and reduce hormone levels, or surgery (thyroidectomy) to remove the thyroid for a permanent solution. After surgery, patients will need to take synthetic thyroid replacement hormones for life.

faqs.q: What is the most common type of endocrine disorder?
faqs.a: Diabetes is the most common endocrine disorder, occurring when the pancreas fails to produce enough insulin or the body cannot use insulin effectively, leading to elevated blood sugar levels.

faqs.q: Why Choose Yashoda Hospitals’ Department of Endodontics?
faqs.a: Specialized Root Canal Expertise: The team includes leading endodontists specializing in complex, routine, traumatic RCTs and are experts at handling degenerative issues like internal/external resorption.

faqs.q: What happens if an infected tooth is not treated?
faqs.a: Dental abscesses won’t go away on their own, and germs from the tooth can spread to the jawbone, sinuses, and tissues around the tooth if they aren’t addressed. This trend typically leads to serious problems such as bone damage, chronic sinusitis, or facial cellulitis that may need surgical treatment. Under the worst of situations, the infection can bring about life-threatening systemic illnesses such as sepsis or airway obstructions, which require rapid healthcare from an emergency specialist.

faqs.q: Is microscopic root canal treatment available at Yashoda Hospital?
faqs.a: Endodontists at Yashoda Hospitals of HITEC city, and Secunderabad are equipped with advanced microscope-assisted endodontics to correctly detect hidden canals, treat complicated root structures, and dramatically enhance overall success rates for complex RCTs.

faqs.q: Why choose Yashoda Hospital, Hyderabad, for endodontic treatment?
faqs.a: Yashoda Hospital brings together dental surgeons, endodontists, and maxillofacial experts under one roof, which is particularly helpful for patients with underlying health concerns, such as diabetes or heart disease, which can complicate treatment. We also employ current instruments such as laser dentistry, digital X-rays, and minimally invasive procedures, and our rigorous sterilization practices help patients heal more quickly, feel less pain, and have a decreased risk of problems following surgery.

faqs.q: What should I do if I have pus, swelling, fever, or a bad taste in a tooth?
faqs.a: If you are experiencing these symptoms, please take it as a dental emergency and get attention right away. Ask for an emergency appointment, and if you are having difficulty breathing or swallowing they surgeons may appoint an ER. Meanwhile, gently washing with warm salt water and putting a cold compress to your cheek every 15 minutes might help relieve the discomfort and swelling. Avoid attempting to drain the abscess yourself, as this can exacerbate the infection by pushing it deeper. If you want pain relief, ibuprofen works well; however, avoid placing any unprescribed medications directly on the gum tissue, since this can cause burns.

faqs.q: Can pregnant women get root canal treatment?
faqs.a: Yes, pregnant women may safely have root canal treatment, and in many situations, it is actually better to treat the infection rather than leave it untreated, since an uncontrolled tooth infection may represent a bigger risk to the baby than the surgery itself. We normally prefer to do it during the second trimester, when it is the safest and most pleasant, with precautions such as limiting X-ray exposure and avoiding certain drugs. The crucial developmental period that occurs during the first trimester of pregnancy and the terrible pain that occurs during the third trimester are both avoided.

faqs.q: What is root canal retreatment, and when is it needed?
faqs.a: Root canal retreatment is a second operation performed on a previously treated tooth that has not healed correctly or caught a new infection over time. This can happen for several reasons, including a missed canal during the initial treatment, a breakdown of the inner sealing material, a new cavity that allowed bacteria to re-enter the tooth, swelling that appears after a month, or delayed restoration. In most cases, retreatment can save the tooth rather than extraction.

faqs.q: How long does a typical root canal appointment take?
faqs.a: A normal root canal treatment lasts between 30 and 90 minutes, with milder cases on front teeth generally completed in a single session, but difficult or seriously infected molars with many canals might take 60 to 90 minutes or more and may necessitate a second visit for final restoration. The total length is determined by criteria such as which tooth is being treated, the complexity of the root canal system, and whether a temporary or permanent filling is being inserted that day. Most patients find the operation to be considerably more painless than they imagined, owing to the local anesthetic, while some slight stiffness after the numbness wears off is quite common.

faqs.q: What are the symptoms of sinusitis?
faqs.a: Sinusitis, or a sinus infection, presents with symptoms such as facial pain, nasal congestion with thick mucus, postnasal drip, and a persistent cough that often worsens at night. Other signs may include headaches, fever, sore throat, reduced sense of smell, and general fatigue or weakness.

faqs.q: What causes ear infections in adults?
faqs.a: Ear infections in adults can be caused by various factors, including bacterial and viral infections, which can lead to middle ear infections. Blockages in the eustachian tube, often due to colds or allergies, can prevent fluid drainage and promote infection. Other causes include swimming, which may introduce bacteria into the ear canal (known as swimmer’s ear), minor injuries from cleaning ears or using in-ear headphones, and viral illnesses like the flu.

faqs.q: Why is it performed?
faqs.a: Endoscopy is an essential diagnostic and therapeutic procedure in the gastroenterology department due to its capacity to directly observe and address digestive tract issues, including providing an accurate diagnosis for various conditions, tissue sampling, and minor procedures like controlled bleeding and polyp removal.

faqs.q: Why is it performed?
faqs.a: Colonoscopy is a versatile procedure to diagnose and treat a variety of gastrointestinal disorders, including evaluation of an entire colon, polyp removal and colon cancer screening. A colonoscopy sets up a golden standard for diagnosing and managing colorectal cancers, as well as treating certain conditions such as bowel strangulation, diverticulitis, and inflammatory bowel diseases (IBD).

faqs.q: Why is it performed?
faqs.a: Endoscopic Retrograde Cholangiopancreatography (ERCP) is a unique combination of procedures that involves imaging techniques combined with direct field of vision and surgical interventions within the bile and pancreatic ducts. ERCP is a versatile procedure to diagnose and treat various conditions related to these ducts, including gallstones, tumours, and strictures.

faqs.q: Why is it performed?
faqs.a: Its non-invasive nature provides a painless and quick assessment of liver fibrosis, as it measures the presence and severity of liver stiffness and fat accumulation (steatosis), an important indicator of liver health. It is a valuable tool for providing timely diagnosis and monitoring of various liver diseases, including liver cirrhosis, viral hepatitis, and non-alcoholic fatty liver disease (NAFLD).

faqs.q: Why is it performed?
faqs.a: HRM can provide comprehensive and goal-oriented assessments for various oesophageal motility disorders, like achalasia and GERD, leading to improved diagnostic precision and making the interpretation of diseases easier.

faqs.q: Why is it performed?
faqs.a: Endoscopic ultrasound provides live, high-resolution images of the GI tract and its surrounding organs, allowing comprehensive and personalised evaluation of diseases and guiding treatments like biopsies. It imposes a direct viewing angle at high proximity to the targeted tissues and ensures accurate diagnosis and staging for the various conditions.

faqs.q: Why is it performed?
faqs.a: Oesophageal pH monitoring is a gold standard for measuring gastroesophageal reflux (GER) and diagnosing GERD by providing a direct measurement of acid exposure which aids in correlating symptoms with reflux episodes and assessing the effectiveness of the treatment.

faqs.q: What does a gastroenterologist do?
faqs.a: A gastroenterologist is a medical specialist who focuses on the diagnosis, treatment, prevention, and management of disorders related to the gastrointestinal tract and associated organs such as the liver, gallbladder, and pancreas. In addition, they obtain medical histories, conduct comprehensive physical examinations, and assess the liver and bile ducts. Preview: Laparoscopic-assisted vaginal hysterectomy aims to remove the uterus and sometimes ovaries, allowing the surgeons to perform it vaginally through a laparoscopic technique while minimizing the procedure’s invasiveness. It is frequently employed to treat a variety of abdominal conditions, including fibroids, abnormal uterine bleeding, and uterine prolapse.

Preview: Total laparoscopic hysterectomy (TLH) aims to treat various gynecological conditions by removing the uterus in cases such as uterine fibroids, endometriosis, and abnormal uterine bleeding. Sometimes, its purpose is to prevent or manage cancer, control pain and bleeding, and consensually prevent future pregnancies.
Preview: Vulvectomy aims to remove all or the affected parts of the vulva, including its surrounding areas or lymph nodes, to treat vulvar cancers and prevent the spread of precancerous conditions.

faqs.q: Why is it performed?
faqs.a: Ultrasound, also known as sonography or USG, visualizes and assesses the female reproductive organs, such as the uterus, ovaries, and fallopian tubes, to identify fibroids, polyps, and other structural abnormalities and detect cysts, tumours, and ovarian reserves. It’s commonly used to diagnose and monitor, and as a guidance procedure during chronic pelvic pain, abnormal bleeding, fertility issues, and pregnancy complications.

faqs.q: Why is it performed?
faqs.a: Fluoroscopy aims to visualize and assess the female reproductive organs, like the uterus and fallopian tubes, during procedures like hysterosalpingography (HSG) to diagnose infertility, assess tubal patency (whether the fallopian tubes are open or blocked), and fallopian tube recanalization.

faqs.q: Why is it performed?
faqs.a: It aims to assess the well-being of the fetus during pregnancy and labour by detecting abnormal fetal heart rate or any fetal distress. It can be used to constantly monitor the fetus and prevent adverse outcomes like stillbirth, neonatal morbidity, and mortality.

faqs.q: Why is it performed?
faqs.a: Its modern approach enhances precision, visualization, and minimally invasive techniques, making complex surgeries like myomectomy , hysterectomy , and oophorectomy more effective and time-efficient.

faqs.q: Why is it performed?
faqs.a: Advanced labour rooms provide specialized care during childbirth by aiming to reduce maternal and newborn mortality and morbidity. They are well-equipped for normal and complex delivery procedures and specialised neonatal support.

faqs.q: Why is it performed?
faqs.a: Advanced maternity suites provide personalized care to women throughout their pregnancy, childbirth and postpartum period, focusing specifically on their unique motherly needs. It involves all the prenatal checkups, delivery services and postpartum care, including the management of ectopic pregnancy or hyperemesis gravidarum.

faqs.q: Why is it performed?
faqs.a: Minimally Invasive Surgical Units (MISU), or minimal access gynaecological surgery (MAGS), aim for a less invasive alternative by utilizing specialized instruments and techniques (laparoscopy, hysteroscopy , and robotic surgery ). Commonly treated conditions using these units are uterine prolapse, ectopic pregnancy, endometriosis, fibroids, and ovarian cysts.

faqs.q: Why is it performed?

Eligibility to apply for various conditions

Preview: A syngeneic transplant aims to restore healthy blood production and immunological function by replacing damaged marrow with cancer-free cells from an identical twin. The donor and recipient are genetically identical; this approach successfully reduced the danger of graft rejection and graft-versus-host disease while allowing for extensive conditioning treatments to destroy residual cancer cells.

The remaining blood components are restored into the patient within the last four to six hours of this non-surgical procedure. The extracted cells are processed and cryopreserved for future treatments.

Preview: High-dose chemotherapy seeks to destroy aggressive cancer cells and establish long-term remission in diseases such as lymphoma and multiple myeloma. It also works to ablate damaged bone marrow and inhibit the immune system, allowing fresh stem cells to engraft without rejection.
the entire body in multiple portions over several days, giving healthy tissues time to recuperate between treatments.

faqs.q: Why is it performed?
faqs.a: Negative pressure chambers keep the hospital safe by drawing air in and filtering it through HEPA systems to remove infectious pathogens like TB or measles. Positive-pressure ventilation rooms, on the other hand, protect immunocompromised individuals by forcing filtered, sterile air outward to keep external toxins and dust out.

faqs.q: Why is it performed?
faqs.a: Dedicated labs use sterile, controlled systems and HEPA-filtered settings to prevent microbial contamination during complex processes such as genetic modification. These facilities follow high-quality control standards, ensuring that each batch of cells meets tight regulatory purity, viability, and potency criteria before being used on patients.

faqs.q: Why is it performed?
faqs.a: Cryopreservation prevents metabolic activity to preserve biological materials for a variety of applications, including preserving fertility throughout medical treatments and banking life-saving stem cells and tissues for transplantation. This approach also preserves the genetic variety of endangered animals and agricultural resources for future study and conservation efforts. It serves as an important link between data collection and therapeutic applications, like bone marrow and its compatibility

faqs.q: Why is it performed?
faqs.a: Bone marrow transplantation is a life-saving procedure for patients with severe malignancies, severe blood diseases, and inherited immunological deficiencies that prevent the body from manufacturing healthy blood cells.

faqs.q: Why is it performed?
faqs.a: Apheresis machines are used for a key therapeutic technique that removes unwanted cells, antibodies, or poisons from a patient’s circulation while also efficiently collecting high-demand blood components from donors. This adaptable treatment is used to treat a variety of illnesses, including rapidly lowering excessive white blood cell counts in leukemia, swapping red cells in sickle cell disease, and eliminating nerve-attacking antibodies in neurological disorders.

faqs.q: Why is it performed?
faqs.a: Blood component irradiation employs gamma or X-rays to destroy donor T cell DNA, stopping them from attacking immunocompromised recipients’ organs. This crucial procedure is largely necessary for high-risk patients, such as stem cell transplant recipients and those with significant immunodeficiencies, to prevent the deadly transfusion-related Graft-versus-Host Disease (TA-GVHD). It guarantees that vulnerable groups receive safe transfusions.

faqs.q: Why is it performed?
faqs.a: Under an expert’s guidance, infusion pumps provide for accurate administration of powerful medications such as opioids and insulin, as well as a continuous supply of fluids, nourishment, and chemotherapy over long periods of time. These devices are vital in critical and palliative care settings for safely giving life-saving sedatives, vasopressors, and regulated pain management.

faqs.q: Why is it performed?
faqs.a: Rapid molecular testing yields quick answers for infectious diseases such as the flu or strep, allowing doctors to begin targeted therapies and isolation measures in minutes. These mobile devices aid, against superbugs and expand high-quality diagnostic capabilities to resource-constrained rural places by detecting particular genetic markers for antibiotic resistance.

pages.title: Best Liver Hospital in Hyderabad, India
overview: Liver diseases are often caused by viral infection or inherited metabolic conditions, such as glycogen storage disorders or Wilson’s disease. Biliary obstructions, intake of alcohol, exposure to industrial toxic chemicals, and cardiovascular diseases, such as right-sided heart failure, occur as the blood flow to & from the liver is interrupted, and certain common conditions, such as obesity, high lipid levels, & high blood pressure, damage the liver.

It is a well-known fact that the liver can tirelessly fight back against diseases, detoxify, and provide an essential nutrient supply to the body; however, like humans, though it is resilient, at one point, the liver fails to function properly when it’s loaded beyond its capacity. However, during this silent battle, the symptoms aren’t noticed in most individuals, & so by the time its symptoms arrive & the diagnosis is carried out, the damage is already existing; therefore, mild symptoms or any suspicions are to be immediately reported to a doctor for early diagnosis to prevent severe complications.

We at Yashoda Hospitals ensure to provide the best liver care through early diagnostics & expert care, managing simple to complex liver diseases under the roof of top hepatologists & a multidisciplinary team with advanced diagnostic tools & cutting-edge facilities. Our team of internationally acclaimed specialists brings extensive experience in treating both pediatric and adult liver diseases. This includes chronic hepatic diseases, cirrhosis, acute liver failure, cholestatic liver diseases, congenital biliary diseases, inherited metabolic disorders, liver cancers, and bile duct cancers.

From minor ailments like tiredness and abdominal pain to severe liver damage, such as fatty liver & cirrhosis, all are handled with the best seamless, personalized care at Yashoda Hospitals, aiming for the highest level of precision and excellence from the initial evaluation to post-surgery follow-up to ensure a smooth and stress-free experience for the patients.
Leaders in the Hepatology Department: Yashoda Hospitals, recognized as the best hospital for hepatology in Hyderabad, offers top-notch services and a patient-centric approach.

It is a well-known fact that the liver can tirelessly fight back against diseases, detoxify, and provide an essential nutrient supply to the body; however, like humans, though it is resilient, at one point, the liver fails to function properly when it’s loaded beyond its capacity. However, during this silent battle, the symptoms aren’t noticed in most individuals, & so by the time its symptoms arrive & the diagnosis is carried out, the damage is already existing; therefore, mild symptoms or any suspicions are to be immediately reported to a doctor for early diagnosis to prevent severe complications.

faqs.q: Why Choose Yashoda Hospitals for Hepatology?
faqs.a: Leaders in the Hepatology Department: Yashoda Hospitals, recognized as the best hospital for hepatology in Hyderabad, offers top-notch services and a patient-centric approach.

faqs.q: What are the four warning signs of a damaged liver?
faqs.a: The liver has a high regeneration capacity, and so symptoms typically do not appear early on. However, yellowing of the eyes and skin, swelling and discomfort in the abdomen, severe fatigue, light-colored stools, and dark urine are four warning signs not to be ignored, as they indicate potential damage to the liver.

faqs.q: Who is the best doctor to see for liver disease?
faqs.a: Hepatologists, also known as liver specialists, are experts in diagnosing and treating conditions associated with the liver, gallbladder, bile ducts, and pancreas, and they enhance liver function through top-notch care utilizing the latest advancements. Therefore, when you experience any of the liver disease symptoms, it is best to consult a hepatologist.

faqs.q: What are the common liver conditions treated at Yashoda Hospitals?
faqs.a: The hepatology department at Yashoda Hospitals treats certain diseases & conditions associated with organs like the liver, gallbladder, bile ducts, and pancreas, which include viral hepatitis, alcohol-related liver diseases, fatty liver, cirrhosis, acute or chronic liver failure, liver tumors, biliary cholangitis, and some uncommon genetic disorders such as Wilson’s disease (liver damage resulting from iron accumulation).

faqs.q: What is a FibroScan liver?
faqs.a: While liver function tests or blood tests are commonly used to diagnose liver diseases and assess liver enzyme levels, they do not accurately identify the specific stage of liver disease. To address this limitation, advanced technologies like FibroScan, which uses ultrasound imaging, are employed to assess the stiffness and scarring of liver tissue, thereby preventing disease progression and related complications.

faqs.q: What tests confirm liver disease?
faqs.a: To assess the functioning of the liver, it’s important to evaluate the levels of enzymes involved, as well as to examine any structural or internal damage resulting from infections or alcohol consumption. Various blood tests, including the bilirubin test, SGOT, and SGPT, in combination with liver biopsies and advanced diagnostic methods like FibroScan, along with standard imaging techniques, such as CT and MRI scans, are typically used to diagnose liver diseases.

faqs.q: Is liver disease curable?
faqs.a: While mild liver conditions like alcoholic and toxin-induced liver diseases are often curable, some conditions, such as non-alcoholic liver diseases, can be managed. However, more severe liver issues, including cirrhosis, autoimmune hepatitis, and many liver cancers, cannot be completely cured, and in these instances, a liver transplant may be a viable option to slow disease progression.

faqs.q: What lifestyle changes help liver health?
faqs.a: Achieving and maintaining a healthy weight through a nutritious, protein-rich, and balanced diet, along with adequate sleep and exercise, drinking sufficient water, and eliminating habits like smoking or alcohol consumption, are crucial lifestyle changes that can promote liver health.

faqs.q: Why Choose Yashoda Hospitals for Infectious Diseases?
faqs.a: Leading Center for Infectious Diseases: Yashoda Hospitals is recognized as the best hospital for infectious disease control in India, offering top-notch antimicrobial treatment and care.

faqs.q: What are the common symptoms of infectious diseases?
faqs.a: The common symptoms associated with various infectious diseases include fever, sore throat, mild to severe cough, nasal congestion, chills, breathing difficulty, abdominal pain, nausea, vomiting, & other related issues.

faqs.q: How do infectious diseases spread?
faqs.a: The typical ways infectious diseases are transmitted include inhaling infected respiratory droplets or airborne particles, consuming contaminated food and water, touching contaminated surfaces, or encountering vectors like ticks and mosquitoes.

faqs.q: How do you diagnose an infectious disease?
faqs.a: The assessment involves a physical examination, along with reviewing medical and travel history, conducting laboratory tests, and performing imaging tests like CT scans, MRIs, and biopsies as a comprehensive approach to diagnosing an infectious disease.

faqs.q: Which doctors treat infectious diseases?
faqs.a: Infectious specialists are medical experts who are specialized in the diagnosis, treatment, & prevention of infectious diseases caused by various pathogens such as bacteria, viruses, fungi, & parasites.

faqs.q: When should I consult an infectious disease specialist?
faqs.a: If you have a fever accompanied by chills or fatigue, or observe any non-healing wounds, or if you’ve encountered abdominal problems after recent travel that aren’t improving with medication, it’s essential to consult an infectious disease specialist.

faqs.q: Can all infectious diseases be cured?
faqs.a: Although many infections can be treated effectively, certain conditions like HIV and hepatitis can be managed with medication and vaccines, but they cannot be entirely cured.

faqs.q: How to control infectious diseases?
faqs.a: Practicing good hygiene, such as thoroughly washing hands, avoiding clear or direct contact with infected individuals, keeping informed about vaccinations, and minimizing the chances of bites or encounters with certain domestic animals, are some steps to be taken to prevent infectious diseases.

faqs.q: Why Choose Yashoda for Interventional Radiology in Hyderabad?

faqs.q: How is interventional radiology different from traditional surgery?
faqs.a: Interventional radiology uses live imaging (X-ray, CT, or MRI) to guide tiny catheters through 2-3 mm punctures, giving a more precise alternative to standard surgery’s extensive incisions and tissue damage. By putting local anesthetic and focused navigation ahead of general anesthesia and open operating durations, these treatments reduce patient risk and scarring while allowing for significantly speedier recovery.

faqs.q: Can interventional radiology be used for cancer treatment?
faqs.a: As the “fourth pillar” of cancer therapy, interventional radiology uses CT or MRI guidance to administer targeted therapies such as TACE, radiofrequency ablation, and embolization directly to tumors while sparing healthy tissue. At Yashoda Hospitals, these minimally invasive procedures, such as TACE for liver cancer and RFA for renal cell carcinoma, successfully eliminate malignancies and control symptoms while causing much less trauma than standard surgery.

faqs.q: What are the benefits of interventional radiology compared to surgery?
faqs.a: Interventional Radiology provides a highly precise and minimally invasive alternative to traditional surgery by guiding instruments through tiny pinhole entries using real-time imaging such as CT, ultrasound, and MRI. This approach greatly decreases tissue trauma, pain, and the risks associated with general anesthesia. Most patients benefit from same-day release, which allows them to resume their everyday lives much more quickly with lower medical expenses. These creative methods provide safe alternatives for treating cancer and vascular disease.

pages.title: Best Kidney Transplant Hospital in Hyderabad/India
overview: Kidneys process about 180 litres of blood and remove almost 2 litres of waste every day. Kidney diseases and injuries can affect how kidneys function. Over a period of time, untreated kidney diseases can result in a slow and silent decline in kidney function. Some of the diseases, such as diabetic kidney disease, blood pressure, glomerular diseases, congenital kidney diseases, trauma, or poisoning, can cause damage to the nephrons, the filtering units of kidneys, and thus reduce the kidney function or renal function.

Renal function or kidney function is intact when both the kidneys are working efficiently, as measured by the estimated glomerular filtration rate (eGFR). eGFR is the measure of the percentage of kidney function that is available. Rarely, a small or mild decline (30 to 40%) is noticed even in normal individuals. People with reduced kidney function usually have an associated kidney disease , and there are chances of both of them worsening with age. Kidney diseases and decline in kidney function are usually silent, until the decline is severe with an eGFR of 25%. Any further decline below 10–15% requires lifetime dialysis or renal transplant.

India boasts one of the highest kidney transplant success rates globally, with over 90% success reported for the approximately 7,500 kidney transplants performed annually. Currently, 90% of these transplants come from living donors, while only 10% are from deceased donors, typically patients who have passed away due to brain stroke or accidents.

Our kidney transplant centres are situated at 3 locations, offering a wide range of treatment options for kidney diseases with a success rate of 90%. Our kidney transplant program is aimed at providing quality services for patients with end-stage kidney disease and chronic kidney disease.

Acute Kidney Injury (AKI) is a sudden drop in kidney function caused by infection, trauma, or an accident. In some cases, the damage to the kidneys can be reversed, which is why it’s also known as acute renal failure. However, if not treated, AKI can lead to permanent kidney damage.

Chronic Kidney Disease (CKD): A long-term condition where the kidneys gradually lose their ability to function properly. This slow decline in kidney function often goes unnoticed until it’s advanced. In CKD, the kidney function, measured by eGFR, falls below 60 for three months or longer.
