faqs.q: Why is it performed?
faqs.a: Yashoda Hospitals utilises minimally invasive procedures like endobronchial ultrasound (EBUS), which aim to accurately diagnose and treat a high volume of patients with various lung diseases, including cancer, infection, and inflammation, ensuring optimal outcomes.

faqs.q: Why is it performed?
faqs.a: A fungal culture test aims to identify the presence of fungal microorganisms thriving in moist, dark environments like skin folds or shoes within the human body.

faqs.q: Why is it performed?
faqs.a: A gallium-67 scan uses an isotope called gallium-67 with SPECT imaging technology to assess blood flow in various organs, study transmetallation and organification in bones and kidneys, determine the functioning of the kidneys, bone marrow, liver and lungs, and identify infections and cancers throughout the body.

faqs.q: Why is it performed?
faqs.a: The Mantoux test, also known as the Pirquet or tuberculin sensitivity test, is a skin test performed for the screening of tuberculosis by detecting the presence and amount of localized swelling as a sign of immune response against Mycobacterium tuberculosis.

faqs.q: Why is it performed?
faqs.a: The acid-fast bacilli culture test identifies the infections caused by acid-fast bacilli, primarily targeting the lungs for tuberculosis (TB). TB is either represented as latent, where the bacteria are without symptoms and aren’t contagious, or active, where the bacteria show symptoms and are contagious. This test detects active TB and reduces its morbidity and mortality.

faqs.q: Why is it performed?
faqs.a: The adenosine deaminase test measures ADA enzyme levels in various body fluids, particularly pleural fluid or serum, which are elevated as an immune response to tuberculosis, thus giving us an early diagnosis of the disease.

faqs.q: Why is it performed?
faqs.a: An arterial blood gas test (ABG) aims to analyze the levels of oxygen, carbon dioxide, oxygen saturation and bicarbonate in the bloodstream to assess lung function and maintain the body’s crucial pH balance.

faqs.q: Why is it performed?
faqs.a: A pulmonary function test (PFT) is a non-invasive procedure that measures large volume, capacity and flow rates, aiming to assess the functionality of the lungs. Its purpose is to diagnose and monitor respiratory diseases, guide treatment planning, and analyze a series of abnormalities.

pages.title: Radiation Oncology Hospital in Hyderabad
overview: The Department of Radiation Oncology at Yashoda Cancer Institute is a Centre of Excellence in the country for stereotactic treatments. It is on par with international standards and has one of the most technologically advanced clinical radiation therapy programs in the world. In addition to a large team of specialized doctors and state-of-the-art equipment, the department has highly trained and board-certified radiation oncologists, physicists, dosimetrists, radiation therapists, nurses, dieticians, and other professionals in the evaluation, planning, delivery, and ongoing review of each patient’s treatment. This ensures high-quality comprehensive radiation treatment.

Cancer treatment involves discussion among a board of specialists who decide the best course of treatment. Radiation may be used as a stand-alone treatment or in combination with surgery, chemotherapy, or biological therapy.

The multidisciplinary tumor boards at the Institute meet every week to discuss complex clinical cases in the hospital as well as any other peripheral hospitals. Apart from the surgical, medical, and radiation oncologists, the tumor board is supported by the faculty of other specialties like ENT , neurology , neurosurgery, gastroenterology , urology , pulmonary medicine, and rehabilitative services such as physiotherapy , occupational therapy, stoma care, and speech therapy.

The Department of Radiotherapy at the Institute is equipped with Asia’s first Varian RapidArc Linear Accelerator, which has a sophisticated 3D planning system. It is one of the busiest academic departments in the country.

The technology used in RapidArc represents an exponential leap forward in the speed, power, and precision of radiation therapy. It is capable of delivering radiation at a faster dose rate than conventional linear accelerators. Other special features improve the accuracy of treatment so that the overall duration of a course of treatment may also be shortened from several weeks to just a few days. The technology also enables more effective treatment of tumors in locations that were previously difficult to treat because of the sensitivity of the surrounding tissue to radiation.

Other special features improve the accuracy of treatment so that the overall duration of a course of treatment may also be shortened from several weeks to just a few days. The technology also enables more effective treatment of tumors in locations that were previously difficult to treat because of the sensitivity of the surrounding tissue to radiation.

Yashoda Cancer Institute takes pride in presenting the flattening filter-free (FFF) beam. It is a significant step as it will make the breakthrough technology more accessible and affordable to people across India.

Health Blogs for Cancer

Cancer is a large group of diseases resulting from the uncontrolled division of cells triggered by various reasons. Depending on the organ in which it occurs, cancer gets its name. Cervical cancer occurs in the cervix, the region at the end of the uterus connecting it to the vagina.
Doctor Talk
Health Talk
Diseases & Treatments

faqs.q: Is radiation therapy painful?
faqs.a: External beam radiation therapy (EBRT) is painless because the radiation machine never touches you. As you lie on the treatment table, you may hear a buzzing sound when the machine is active, but you won’t feel anything during the procedure.

faqs.q: What is radiation therapy?
faqs.a: Radiation therapy, or radiotherapy, is a cancer treatment that uses high doses of radiation to destroy cancer cells and reduce tumor size. It can also help alleviate cancer-related symptoms like pain, difficulty swallowing or breathing, and bowel obstructions.

faqs.q: What is the success rate of radiation therapy?
faqs.a: The success rate of radiation therapy is generally high for early-stage cancers but decreases as the stage advances. Factors influencing success include cancer type, tumor location and size, patient age and health, treatment plan, response to treatment, and patient preferences.

faqs.q: How do you reduce the side effects of radiation therapy?
faqs.a: Some of the ways to reduce the side effects are protecting skin by avoiding sun exposure, wearing sunscreen, and opting for loose-fitting clothing made from natural fibers. Additionally, it is crucial to maintain a healthy diet, consider food supplements, get plenty of rest, take short walks, avoid shaving or using hair removal products on the treated area, and be cautious when swimming in chlorinated pools.

faqs.q: How is radiation therapy done?
faqs.a: Radiation therapy, or radiotherapy, can be delivered through external beam radiation or internal radiation. External beam radiation uses a linear accelerator to focus high-energy beams on the treatment area, usually in painless sessions over several days or weeks. Internal radiation, like brachytherapy, involves placing a radioactive source inside the body near cancerous cells, either temporarily or for extended periods. The choice between these methods depends on the type and location of the cancer, tumor size, and the patient’s overall health.

faqs.q: How does radiation therapy work?
faqs.a: Radiation therapy, or radiotherapy, treats cancer by using high doses of radiation to damage the DNA of cancer cells. This damage prevents the cells from dividing and causes them to die. The body then naturally breaks down and eliminates the damaged cells.

faqs.q: Is radiotherapy safe?
faqs.a: Radiotherapy is a well-established, safe, and effective treatment used to cure or manage symptoms of cancers like head and neck, brain, breast, cervical, prostate, and skin cancer. Millions of patients undergo radiotherapy each year.

Yashoda Institute of Radiology and Imaging is a synergy of the advanced radio-imaging facilities, skilled radiologists and technicians, so as to offer, the referring doctors and the patients, an overall assessment of suspected diseases or anomalies.

Diagnostic radiologists make use of latest technological advances in radiation, magnetism, and sound waves to explore disease-causing structural changes or defects. Yashoda Institute of Radiology and Imaging supports physicians and surgeons across multidisciplinary institutes in Yashoda Hospitals with radiology and imaging diagnostics such as 3-Tesla MRI , 64 – slice CT, vascular doppler, ultrasonography , digital X-ray (roentgenography), 3D mammography, myelography and fluoroscopy .

The radiologists closely work with specialty clinicians to ensure all the required information is interpreted from the diagnostic data. Also, the team works together to reduce the possible risks and complications that are related to the procedure.

Intra-operative MRI and virtual procedures such as 3D virtual bronchoscopy, 3D virtual colonoscopy, have paved new paths towards unprecedented surgical outcomes especially in neurosurgery and pulmonary surgery divisions. These intra-operative diagnostic procedures not only offer surgeon the ease and better surgical-site approach but also an array of benefits to the patient. Improved surgical corrections in a single sitting, lesser complications, smaller incisions and scar, faster recovery and sooner return to the normal routine are some of the patient-centric benefits. Also with technical advances, in many cases these days, radiological procedures successfully obviate the need for invasive procedures.

sectionHighlights: Medical radiology and imaging technology are an irreplaceable component of modern diagnostic services. Yashoda Institute of Radiology and Imaging is a synergy of the advanced radio-imaging facilities, skilled radiologists and technicians, so as to offer, the referring doctors and the patients, an overall assessment of suspected diseases or anomalies.
Best in class technologies and facilities:

Dual Source Dual Energy CT – Computed tomography (CT) scan offers a detailed, high-quality, 360-degree or cross-sectional image of human anatomy. The radiologists control the equipment and X-ray beams to scan soft tissues, bones and internal organs to identify abnormalities such as injuries, infection, tumor, and degeneration. Dual source CT scan uses two sources of X-ray and helps to detect diseases faster with reduced exposure to radiation. This is extremely useful for diseases of blood vessels.

Intra-operative MRI – Surgeons and radiologists integrate diagnosis and therapeutic interventions with great care to give patients the best possible outcomes from the surgeries, especially heart surgeries and brain surgeries. Yashoda Hospitals being the first in India with Intra-operative MRI ( iMRI ) is the pioneer of this cutting-edge diagnostic technology.

Ultrasonography – Ultrasonography uses sound waves to produce an image of internal organs and structures. It is used for diagnosis of diseases affecting organs in any part of the body, especially in breast imaging and echocardiogram. Ultrasonography is also used as guidance for biopsies. Usually noninvasive, however, a probe may be used for transesophageal echocardiogram, transrectal ultrasound and transvaginal ultrasound.
2D and 3D Digital mammography – Digital mammography (tomosynthesis)coupled with 3D technology allows oncologists and gynecologists to evaluate for tumors (and cancer) and also guide biopsies.

Digital X-ray – The utility of X-ray sensors gives digital X-ray an upper hand over conventional X-ray in terms of enhanced quality image and cost-effectiveness. Digital X-ray may be used to detect bone injuries and diseases, digestive problems and many more health conditions.
Dual-energy X-ray absorptiometry (DEXA) – DEXA measures bone mineral density and thus bone loss by using two beams of X-ray. It is useful in the diagnosis of bone diseases such as osteoporosis.
Stress X-ray and arthrography to evaluate bones and joints for fractures, infection, inflammation, deformities, injuries and diseases such as arthritis
Early diagnosis of neurological disorders such as Alzheimer’s diseases
Evaluating diseases (or damage) of organs in digestive, renal, respiratory, circulatory and endocrine system

At Yashoda Institute of Radiology & Imaging, it is the expert medical team, state-of-the-art facilities and patient care with individualized diagnosis and treatment plans that enable us to achieve high success rates. The team of specialists here at the Yashoda Institute of Radiology & Imaging, comprehensively evaluate health to create an individualized plan of care.

faqs.q: What is a radiology diagnostic test?
faqs.a: A radiology diagnostic test or diagnostic imaging test is a medical procedure that uses advanced techniques and cutting-edge machinery to create images of body parts from within the body to diagnose illness and injury. Examples include X-rays, CT scans, MRI scans, PET scans, and Ultrasound.

faqs.q: What diseases do radiologists treat?
faqs.a: Radiologists treat a wide range of diseases and injuries, using diagnostic imaging tests, such as cancers, heart diseases, bone fractures, lung diseases, dental problems, and uterine disorders.

faqs.q: What is intra-operative diagnosis?
faqs.a: It is the most vital part of any surgery as it quickly determines the underlying issues and often guides during any surgery to determine malignant or benign pathology. Here are some techniques performed under intra-operative diagnosis: Frozen Section or Crush Cytology.

faqs.q: What can radiology cause?
faqs.a: Radiation from these radiologic examinations can damage DNA and increase the risk of developing cancer. Prolonged exposure to this radiation can also cause skin irritation, cataracts, and hair loss. In some instances, exposure to high doses of radiation may even result in birth abnormalities.

faqs.q: What is the latest technology in radiology?
faqs.a: Yashoda Hospitals offers the best in class technologies and facilities such as Dual Source Dual Energy CT, 3T MRI and Intra-operative MRI, Ultrasonography, 3D Digital mammography, Dual-energy X-ray absorptiometry (DEXA), and Digital X-ray.

CAPD training facilities for end-stage renal failure patients

Candidates for a kidney transplant typically have conditions like end-stage renal disease (ESRD) or kidney failure, are fit for major surgery, can take required medications, have a life expectancy of at least five years, and understand postoperative care. Each hospital may have specific criteria for transplant recipients.

Non-surgical treatments for kidney conditions, such as kidney stones, include endoscope removal to break or remove stones, ultrasound shock wave therapy to break up smaller stones for natural passage, and observation for stones that may pass on their own. These methods often avoid the need for surgery or hospitalization.
What are the most common renal surgeries?

Common renal surgeries include Nephrectomy, which can be partial (removing part of the kidney) or radical (removing the entire kidney along with surrounding tissues). Other procedures include Lithotripsy (using shock waves to break kidney stones), PCNL (removing large stones), Laparoscopic cyst ablation (removing kidney cysts), and Laparoscopic pyeloplasty (correcting ureter-kidney attachment issues).
Diseases & Treatments

faqs.q: What is the purpose of a renal biopsy?
faqs.a: A renal biopsy, or kidney biopsy, typically takes 15 to 30 minutes, with the entire procedure lasting about an hour. This includes numbing the area, using ultrasound or a CT scan to guide the needle, and inserting the needle into the kidney.

faqs.q: How long does a renal biopsy take?
faqs.a: A renal biopsy, or kidney biopsy, typically takes 15 to 30 minutes, with the entire procedure lasting about an hour. This includes numbing the area, using ultrasound or a CT scan to guide the needle, and inserting the needle into the kidney.

faqs.q: What is CRRT?
faqs.a: Continuous renal replacement therapy (CRRT) is a 24-hour, continuous dialysis treatment used for patients with acute kidney injury (AKI) and fluid overload. It may be needed if a child appears swollen or if blood tests show elevated levels of waste products.

faqs.q: Who can get a kidney transplant?
faqs.a: Candidates for a kidney transplant typically have conditions like end-stage renal disease (ESRD) or kidney failure, are fit for major surgery, can take required medications, have a life expectancy of at least five years, and understand postoperative care. Each hospital may have specific criteria for transplant recipients.

faqs.q: Can a kidney be treated without surgery?
faqs.a: Non-surgical treatments for kidney conditions, such as kidney stones, include endoscope removal to break or remove stones, ultrasound shock wave therapy to break up smaller stones for natural passage, and observation for stones that may pass on their own. These methods often avoid the need for surgery or hospitalization.

faqs.q: What is a renal angiogram?
faqs.a: A renal angiogram is an imaging test used to examine the blood vessels in the kidneys. It helps healthcare providers detect issues such as aneurysms (ballooning), stenosis (narrowing), or blockages in the vessels, and assess how well blood is flowing to the kidneys.

faqs.q: What are the most common renal surgeries?
faqs.a: Common renal surgeries include Nephrectomy, which can be partial (removing part of the kidney) or radical (removing the entire kidney along with surrounding tissues). Other procedures include Lithotripsy (using shock waves to break kidney stones), PCNL (removing large stones), Laparoscopic cyst ablation (removing kidney cysts), and Laparoscopic pyeloplasty (correcting ureter-kidney attachment issues).

pages.title: Department of Rheumatology, Yashoda Hospitals, Hyderabad
overview: Rheumatic disease s are the conditions that affect a person’s joint tendons, ligaments, bones, and muscles. They are commonly known as musculoskeletal diseases. The field of medicine that deals with the diagnosis as well as the treatment of these types of conditions are known as rheumatology.

Some of the common symptoms associated with rheumatic conditions are joint pain, stiffness, loss of motion of a joint, inflammation in the joints and surrounding areas. A rheumatologist is a specialist responsible for diagnosing and treating a rheumatic condition, including medications, diet, rest, and stress management.

Yashoda Hospital’s center for Rheumatology for joint diseases is one of the largest centers in Hyderabad for treating all types of rheumatic conditions. Our offerings include a variety of novel treatments, which cater to the healthcare needs of the patients and we aim to provide them the best services available. We not only offer the best treatment strategies and surgeries to our patients but also ensure the complete relief for all their problems. Our wealth of experience and wide-ranging expertise has helped us grow into one of the best rheumatology hospitals in Hyderabad. Yashoda Hospitals offers the best treatment for injuries, joint pains, and other common rheumatic conditions, making us one of the top hospitals in India.

The Center for Rheumatology is committed to the diagnosis and treatment of all patients suffering from diseases of the joints. The center is supported by a well-equipped orthopedic department. We also have a dedicated physiotherapy department to help arthritis patients with the management of their condition. We focus on providing comprehensive care to our patients that involves educating the patient, dietary, and physical activity guidance, physical therapy, medications, intra-articular and soft tissue injections, and functional rehabilitation.

The Institute has some of the best rheumatic surgeons trained by experts and leaders in the field from universally acclaimed universities and hospitals across the globe. The multidisciplinary team consisting of the best rheumatologists, rheumatic surgeons, physical therapists, nurses, and other healthcare professionals work efficiently to optimize patient care and recovery. We are equipped with advanced technologies including MRI scans , CT scans , and world-class infrastructure to provide the latest treatment facilities to treat a wide variety of rheumatic conditions.

Yashoda Institute of Rheumatics is equipped with top-notch technology to advance the delivery of rheumatic care to patients. The facilities and expertise of the diagnosis offered at our hospital have made us one of the best hospitals in Hyderabad. Our actions in rheumatic bone and joint care have put us at the forefront of providing the best treatments and services to our patients.

overview: PRECISION BEYOND LIMITS OF HUMAN HAND Yashoda Hospitals – Institute of Robotic Sciences presents the da Vinci Surgical System, the dawn of new age in minimally invasive surgery. The da Vinci Surgical System powered by state-of-the-art robotic technology is changing the experience of surgery for people around the world. overview: The Institute of Minimally Invasive and Robotic Thoracic Surgery performs a wide range of thoracic surgical procedures on anatomical structures in the chest like the lungs, trachea, esophagus, ribs, diaphragm, pleura, pericardium, nerves, etc using keyhole surgical techniques.

The center has experience in handling lung cancer, esophageal tumors, nerve tumors, chest wall deformities, infective complications due to tuberculosis, airway tumors, chest trauma, and end-stage lung disease needing a lung transplant. Their expertise lies in performing all thoracic surgical procedures using keyhole techniques on Video-assisted Thoracoscopic (VATS) and Robotic (RTS) platforms.
As leaders in the surgical treatment of thoracic diseases, our team of expert thoracic surgeons offers innovative specialized thoracic surgical programs for:

The institute offers cutting-edge screening techniques like Endobronchial ultrasound (EBUS), navigational bronchoscopy, cryobiopsy, and interventional pulmonology. It boasts of the latest in surgical technology in minimally invasive thoracic surgery like Endostaplers, harmonic scalpel, Karl Storz VATS cart, and the Da Vinci Si Robot .

All thoracic surgical patients are managed by a multidisciplinary team of thoracic surgeons, pulmonologists , cardiologists , thoracic intensivists , respiratory therapists, and thoracic anesthesiologists. Support is provided from ancillary specialties like neurology , nephrology , pediatrics , general medicine , and physical medicine and rehabilitation to ensure the best possible care.

Our center is a national tertiary care referral center for complex lung diseases. The lung cancer center at Yashoda is focussed on early detection of lung cancer and surgical management of early lung cancer with subsequent adjuvant therapies like chemotherapy , radiotherapy, and immunotherapy.

pages.title: Department of Spine Surgery, Yashoda Hospitals, Hyderabad
overview: Spinal diseases are conditions that affect the backbone or spinal cord. These diseases include various conditions such as kyphosis , lumbar spine stenosis , spinal muscular atrophy, ankylosing spondylitis , spinal tumors, and many others. These diseases are often caused due to events that directly damage the spine such as trauma to the face, neck, head, and back, excessive sports activities, falling from a height, etc.

The major symptoms that manifest a person suffering from a spine condition are problems in walking, losing control of bladder movements, inability or difficulty in moving arms or legs, headache , stiffness or pain in the neck and back regions, numbness or tingling sensation in the extremities, etc.

faqs.q: What is the amount of risk in laser spine surgery? Which is the best hospital for it in Hyderabad?
faqs.a: Laser spine surgery involves a very small amount of risk depending on the expertise of the surgeon and the condition of the patient. However, with any surgery there is always a small amount of risk of contamination, etc so choosing the best surgeons for this surgery is essential to prevent any major risks.

faqs.q: Is there a difference between endoscopic spine surgery and minimally invasive spine surgery?
faqs.a: Endoscopic surgery is a type of minimally invasive surgery in which the surgeon gains access to the vertebrae by placing a series of tubes to decrease the interruption to the muscles and tissues. It is performed to relieve the pain caused by herniated discs that press the nerve endings.

faqs.q: What is the best hospital for brain tumor surgery in Hyderabad?

faqs.q: What is the duration of spine surgery?
faqs.a: The duration of spine surgery depends on various factors such as the type of procedure, the number of vertebrae involved in the surgery, and the stage or severity of the spinal disease. On average, this surgery can take anywhere between 2 hours to 6-7 hours, and sometimes even longer.

faqs.q: What is spinal fusion surgery?
faqs.a: Spinal fusion surgery is a type of spinal surgery that is aimed at joining two or more vertebrae to stabilize them so that they fuse together to become one. This surgery can be performed on any part of the spine and prevents any movement between the fused vertebrae.

faqs.q: What is lumbar spine surgery?
faqs.a: Lumbar decompression of spine surgery is a type of surgery performed on the lumbar spine region. It is used to treat the compressed nerves in the lumbar or lower spine. This surgery is mainly aimed at improving symptoms such as persistent pain and numbness in the legs that are caused due to pressure on the nerves in the lumbar spine region.

faqs.q: How long does it usually take to recover from spine surgery?
faqs.a: Depending on the type of surgery the surgeon performs on the patient and depending on other factors such as the patient’s age, condition, recovery may take at least 3 to 4 months for the bones to heal properly and in some conditions may also take up to a year to recover.

faqs.q: What is appendicitis? How is it treated?
faqs.a: Appendicitis refers to the inflammation of the appendix present near the abdomen, which causes intense pain.

faqs.q: What are the symptoms of dyspepsia? How is it treated?
faqs.a: The symptoms of dyspepsia include nausea, belching, abdominal pain, bloating, loss of appetite, weight loss, dark stools, inability to swallow food, chest pain, etc.

faqs.q: How is Barrett’s esophagus caused?
faqs.a: The exact cause of this condition is still unknown in medical science. However, a prolonged case of gastroesophageal reflux disease or other related conditions is suspected to cause Barrett’s esophagus.

Preview: It’s a minimally invasive and effective surgical procedure that is similar to open surgery but performed with no incision, primarily in cases of benign prostate hyperplasia (BPH). It aims to remove and preserve prostate tissue for laboratory examination to check for other conditions, including prostate cancer .

Preview: Transurethral resection of the prostate (TURP) is a surgical procedure that aims to remove the excess prostate tissue blocking the urine flow using a resectoscope. Its purpose is to effectively treat moderate to severe urinary issues caused by an enlarged prostate due to BPH or prostate cancer when other treatments fail.

Preview: Cystoscopy and TURBT aim to visualize the urethra and bladder and identify and remove the non-muscle-invasive bladder abnormalities or tumors. Its purpose is to diagnose and treat certain bladder conditions, such as inflammations and cancerous tissues; collect tissue samples; and help determine the extent and stage of the cancer.

faqs.q: Why is it performed?
faqs.a: Cystoscopy helps determine the cause of urinary symptoms and diagnose bladder cancer, bladder stones, infections, and structural problems, as well as monitor the progression or recurrence of the conditions. In rare cases, the surgeons can remove small kidney stones or tumors with cystoscopy.

faqs.q: Why is it performed?
faqs.a: A cystometrogram (CMG) measures bladder pressure to assess how well the bladder stores and releases urine. Its purpose is to provide information about bladder functioning by measuring the pressure changes. It is used to identify overactive bladder, urinary incontinence, or difficulty urinating.

faqs.q: Why is it performed?
faqs.a: A TRUS-guided biopsy is performed to diagnose prostate cancer by utilizing an ultrasound to guide a needle into the prostate gland to detect prostate cancer, assess prostate health, guide treatment decisions, and measure abnormal PSA levels.

faqs.q: Why is it performed?
faqs.a: Urography aims to investigate blood in the urine, diagnose urinary tract conditions, and stage and assess urinary tract cancer. It also helps identify recurrent urinary tract infections as well as examine renal masses.

faqs.q: Why is it performed?
faqs.a: Lithotripsy’s primary aim is to treat kidney stones via a noninvasive or minimally invasive approach to surgery, offering a faster recovery time and reduced risk of complications.

faqs.q: Why is it performed?
faqs.a: Urodynamical laboratory aims to assess the function of the bladder and urethra, which helps diagnose various urinary problems, such as incontinence, overactive bladder, and urinary tension.

faqs.q: Why is it performed?
faqs.a: A uro-oncology unit aims at diagnosing and treating cancers of the urinary and male reproductive system and provides early and accurate diagnosis with specialized expertise and personalized treatments to improve patient outcomes.

faqs.q: Why is it performed?
faqs.a: The pediatric urology unit provides comprehensive care for a wide range of issues, from common conditions such as bedwetting and urinary tract infections to congenital abnormalities.

pages.title: Best Vaccination & Immunization Centre in Hyderabad
overview: Yashoda Hospitals, Hyderabad’s leading center for advanced preventive care, relies on decades of multispecialty expertise in infectious diseases, pediatrics, and immunology to provide evidence-based immunization strategies as well as 24/7 emergency support for routine, travel, and complex high-risk cases. This integrated sub-subspecialty offers life-stage-specific treatment from infancy through geriatrics, using precise, tailored protocols to control disease prevention through an elaborate clinical structure.

Yashoda Hospitals provides world-class vaccination services by combining specialist clinical skills with cutting-edge protocols and innovative storage technology to ensure optimal immunization efficacy for all ages. Our powerful infrastructure manages high-volume programs with a patient-friendly approach, offering custom-made schedules ranging from pediatric care to elderly health. Furthermore, we specialize in negotiating difficult therapeutic pathways, delivering rigorous safety-first policies for immunocompromised patients, skilled management of delayed schedules, and tailored care for special-needs groups.

To provide comprehensive immunization and preventative care, our vaccination clinics rely on knowledge in general medicine, infectious diseases, pediatrics, travel medicine, and occupational health. This collaborative approach involves multidisciplinary case evaluations to significantly increase immunization rates and reduce the prevalence of preventable diseases.

Yashoda Hospitals offers emergency vaccination services available 24/7, which include post-exposure prophylaxis for rabies and tetanus, urgent vaccination for outbreak incidents, immediate immunization counseling for immunocompromised patients, management of adverse events following immunization (AEFI), and emergency travel vaccination consultations. When patients require immediate vaccine-related evaluations and life-saving vaccination procedures, they are provided with prompt access to infectious disease experts, immunologists, allergists, and intensive care units.

Our vaccination center provides a comprehensive treatment plan that includes pre-vaccination screening, allergy assessments, and post-vaccination monitoring to minimize potential adverse effects and ensure safe immunization practices. Ongoing monitoring, including booster dose scheduling, antibody titer testing, personalized immunization plans, and rigorous surveillance procedures to monitor vaccine responses and prevent vaccine-preventable disease transmission, is provided to ensure optimal vaccine efficacy and safety.

Our vaccination and immunization clinic is known for its expertise in managing patients with incomplete immunization histories, vaccine hesitancy, or special medical conditions that require individualized vaccination protocols through thorough history assessments and evidence-based catch-up vaccination schedules. Due to its comprehensive pre-vaccination screening, advanced immunological testing, including antibody titer assessments, and specialized guidance, it is a preferred referral center for immunocompromised patients, pregnant women requiring maternal immunization, travelers to high-risk destinations, and patients with previous adverse vaccine reactions.

Advanced Vaccinations & Immunization Facilities: Our facility has WHO-compliant cold chain systems with digital monitoring, separate emergency vaccination rooms, and modern labs for precise antibody testing.

At Yashoda Hospitals in Hyderabad, our team of infectious disease specialists, immunologists, and vaccinators offers personalized immunization services such as consultations, antibody testing, and treatments at our Institute of Vaccination and Preventive Medicine, which is equipped with WHO-approved cold chain storage facilities and advanced immunological labs.

We offer comprehensive protection against diseases like measles, mumps, rubella (MMR), hepatitis A/B, chickenpox, tetanus, diphtheria, pertussis, pneumococcal infections, influenza, human papillomavirus (HPV), meningococcal disease, and COVID-19, making us one of Hyderabad’s leading specialist preventive health facilities. We offer specialized travel medicine consultations, post-exposure prophylaxis (rabies, hepatitis B, tetanus), vaccine efficacy monitoring by quantitative antibody tests, and occupational health vaccination programs for healthcare workers, the elderly, and pediatric patients with delayed immunization histories.

To protect vulnerable individuals from severe complications and potentially fatal outcomes of infectious diseases, timely and proper vaccination planning remains the most effective long-term strategy for maintaining health and quality of life.

We address the physical and emotional stress often tied to vaccination and utilize our cutting-edge diagnostic tools and patient-approved facilities to ensure every approach is evidence-based and precise, customized for your specific health and job requirements. Whether you require advanced scheduling, destination-specific travel vaccines, or routine adult immunizations, our team offers expert clinical supervision and immunological monitoring required to ensure your longevity and wellness while you work, travel, and live.

Yashoda Group of Hospitals in Hyderabad has provided three decades of exceptional healthcare, blending advanced technology with experienced staff to meet international standards. Their comprehensive international patient services manage everything from visas and travel to insurance, ensuring a seamless and supportive healthcare experience.
What are the common conditions that vaccinations can prevent?
What are the symptoms of vaccine-preventable diseases?

faqs.q: Why Choose Yashoda Hospitals for Immunization and Vaccination?
faqs.a: Best Hospital for Vaccination & Immunization Care: The Centre for General Medicine and Preventive Care employs highly qualified infectious disease experts, immunologists, and vaccinators. We have the best team to handle emergency vaccinations, complicated immunization challenges, and routine vaccine delivery while offering normal pediatric and advanced adult vaccination regimens.

faqs.q: What is vaccination, and how does it work?
faqs.a: Vaccination is the process of putting a disease or one of its parts into a person’s body in a way that weakens or kills it. This triggers the immune system to produce antibodies and memory cells without making the person sick, and it operates by simulating an illness. This trains the immune system to quickly identify and eliminate the actual virus when it emerges, providing long-term protection. Rest, fluids, and over-the-counter painkillers are used to treat common side effects, such as discomfort or moderate fever.

faqs.q: What are the common conditions that vaccinations can prevent?
faqs.a: Getting a vaccine can protect you from serious illnesses like measles, mumps, rubella, polio, diphtheria, tetanus, whooping cough, hepatitis B, the flu, HPV-related cancers, and pneumococcal infections. The bacterial and viral pathogens targeted by these vaccines are those that cause serious consequences, particularly in children and older adults.

faqs.q: What are the symptoms of vaccine-preventable diseases?
faqs.a: Vaccine-preventable infections often include fever, rash, cough, weariness, and respiratory distress; for example, measles causes high fever and Koplik spots, whereas influenza causes sudden chills, body aches, and a sore throat. While polio can cause muscle weakness or paralysis, pertussis is characterized by intense coughing fits.

faqs.q: When should I get vaccinated for the flu?
faqs.a: Get the flu vaccination every year in the early changing seasons (September or October), before peak flu season, as it takes approximately two weeks to establish immunity. It is advised for everyone 6 months and older, especially high-risk populations, including children, pregnant women, and those with chronic diseases.

faqs.q: How is the effectiveness of a vaccine measured?
faqs.a: Vaccine effectiveness (VE) trials compare infection rates between vaccinated and unvaccinated groups, with estimated flu incidence typically ranging from 40-60%, though higher for others, such as measles (97%). Studies conducted in everyday life assess reduced hospitalizations, severe illnesses, and mortality rates.

faqs.q: What treatments are available for vaccine-related side effects?
faqs.a: Mild side effects such as arm soreness, low-grade fever, or tiredness fade on their own; use cold compresses, rest, and over-the-counter medications. Severe responses (e.g., a high temperature >38°C in babies, seizures, or allergy symptoms) demand rapid medical attention.

faqs.q: When should I see a specialist about vaccination concerns?
faqs.a: Consult a professional if your child appears to be very unwell after immunization, has a prolonged high fever, significant fussiness, seizures, or increasing redness/swelling at the injection site. Discuss any second opinions or medical history with your primary care physician before referring to an immunologist for allergies or immunological disorders.
